title: Quiz: Error Mitigation
description: Test your understanding of error mitigation techniques for VQE

questions.question: What does NISQ stand for in quantum computing?
questions.explanation: NISQ stands for Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum, referring to current quantum devices with 50-1000 qubits that have significant error rates but can still perform useful computations.

questions.question: What is the key idea behind zero-noise extrapolation (ZNE)?

options.text: Run circuits with reduced noise using error correction
Amplify noise and extrapolate to the zero-noise limit

questions.explanation: ZNE intentionally amplifies noise (e.g., by gate folding), measures at multiple noise levels, and then extrapolates the results to estimate what the zero-noise value would be.

questions.question: What is unitary folding in the context of ZNE?

options.text: Compressing the circuit to fewer gates
Replacing U with U·U†·U to increase noise

questions.explanation: Unitary folding replaces a gate U with the sequence U·U†·U, which is logically equivalent to U but has 3× the gates, amplifying the noise by approximately a factor of 3.

questions.question: What is the main drawback of probabilistic error cancellation (PEC)?

options.text: It only works for single-qubit gates
The sampling overhead grows exponentially with circuit depth

questions.explanation: PEC's sampling overhead scales as γⁿ where γ > 1 is the cost per gate and n is the number of gates. For deep circuits, this exponential overhead becomes prohibitive.

questions.question: Measurement error mitigation requires inverting a confusion matrix that relates measured outcomes to ideal outcomes.
questions.explanation: True. The confusion matrix M contains probabilities P(measure i | state j). To recover the ideal distribution from measurements, we apply M⁻¹: ideal = M⁻¹ × measured.

questions.question: Why is post-selection on symmetries useful for error mitigation?

options.text: It increases the number of measurements
It allows detection of errors that violate known symmetries
It eliminates the need for any other mitigation
It speeds up the quantum circuit execution

questions.explanation: Physical systems have symmetries (particle number, spin) that must be preserved. When an error occurs, it often violates these symmetries, allowing those results to be detected and discarded.

questions.question: What makes Clifford circuits useful for Clifford Data Regression?

options.text: They can be efficiently simulated classically
They are faster to execute on hardware

questions.explanation: Clifford circuits (composed of H, S, CNOT gates) can be efficiently simulated classically in O(n²) time. This allows computing exact results for training circuits to learn the noise model.

questions.question: Which are common error sources in NISQ devices? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Major NISQ error sources include: two-qubit gate errors (often the dominant source), decoherence (energy relaxation T₁ and dephasing T₂), and measurement errors (incorrect bit readings). Cosmic rays can cause errors but are not a primary concern.
